title: waltheria ovata
intro: Waltheria ovata, also known as the Indian liverwort or bitter leaf, is a plant species in the family Malvaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its bitter and astringent taste, which helps in treating digestive issues, fever, and liver and spleen problems. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Waltheria ovata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat various ailments in traditional African medicine.
